_meta.description: Management Action Plan (MAP) tracker template for NACSA Act 854 CoP compliance audit findings remediation

mapHeader.auditFirm: [Name of audit firm that issued findings]

NotStarted.label: Not Started
NotStarted.description: Remediation actions have not yet commenced. The action item has been acknowledged but work has not begun.

InProgress.label: In Progress
InProgress.description: Remediation actions are actively underway. Work has commenced and is progressing toward the target completion date.

Completed.label: Completed
Completed.description: All remediation actions have been implemented and evidence of completion has been provided. Pending verification by the auditor.
Completed.note: May revert to In Progress if auditor verification identifies incomplete remediation.

Overdue.label: Overdue
Overdue.description: The target completion date has passed and remediation actions are not yet complete. Escalation rules apply.
Overdue.triggerCondition: Automatically applied when current date exceeds target completion date and status is not Completed.

Deferred.label: Deferred
Deferred.description: Remediation has been postponed with documented justification. Requires formal approval from entity management and notification to NACSA for high and critical findings.

actionDescription.summary: [Concise description of the corrective action to be taken]
deliverables: [List of tangible deliverables expected upon completion - e.g., updated policy document, system configuration change, training records]

actionOwner.name: [Name of person accountable for the action]
actionOwner.title: [Job title]

implementer.name: [Name of person performing the work, if different from owner]
implementer.title: [Job title]

escalationContact.name: [Name of person to escalate to if action is blocked or overdue]
escalationContact.title: [Job title]

timeline.dateIdentified: [YYYY-MM-DD - date finding was communicated to entity]
timeline.revisedTargetDate: [YYYY-MM-DD or null - only if original target was formally revised]
timeline.revisionJustification: [Justification for revised target date, if applicable]

progressNotes.author: [Name of person providing update]
progressNotes.note: [Description of progress, challenges, or status change]

evidenceOfCompletion.guidance: Document all evidence that demonstrates the corrective action has been fully implemented.

evidenceItems.evidenceType: [Document | Screenshot | System Export | Configuration File | Training Record | Meeting Minutes | Other]
evidenceItems.fileReference: [File name or reference to evidence storage location]
evidenceItems.providedBy: [Name of person who provided the evidence]

verifiedBy.name: [Name of auditor or reviewer who verified]

verification.verificationMethod: [Document review | System inspection | Re-testing | Interview | Observation]
verification.verificationResult: [Verified - Remediated | Verified - Partially Remediated | Verified - Not Remediated | Pending]

deferralDetails.guidance: Complete this section only if status is DEFERRED.
deferralDetails.deferralJustification: [Detailed justification for why remediation is being deferred]
deferralDetails.compensatingControls: [Description of any interim or compensating controls in place while remediation is deferred]
deferralDetails.riskAcceptanceStatement: [Formal statement of risk acceptance by entity management]

escalationRules.description: Escalation procedures based on finding severity and remediation status. These rules ensure timely remediation and appropriate oversight.

rules.trigger: Critical finding not started within 7 calendar days of MAP creation
rules.action: Formal notification to MAP owner requesting immediate action plan with milestones

rules.trigger: Critical finding overdue by 1-14 calendar days
rules.escalateTo: Chief Executive Officer / Head of Entity
rules.action: Written escalation to entity head with revised timeline and resource allocation plan
rules.nacsaNotificationType: Advisory notification of delay in critical remediation

rules.trigger: Critical finding overdue by more than 14 calendar days
rules.action: Formal notification to NACSA CE. NACSA may direct immediate remediation or impose additional measures under Act 854
rules.nacsaNotificationType: Formal escalation - critical finding remediation failure
rules.regulatoryConsequence: NACSA may exercise powers under Act 854 including directing specific actions or initiating enforcement proceedings

rules.trigger: High finding not started within 14 calendar days of MAP creation
rules.action: Formal notification to MAP owner requesting action commencement with timeline

rules.trigger: High finding overdue by 1-30 calendar days
rules.escalateTo: Chief Executive Officer / Head of Entity
rules.action: Written escalation to entity head with revised timeline and justification for delay

rules.trigger: High finding overdue by more than 30 calendar days
rules.action: Notification to NACSA through updated MAP status report. NACSA may require a follow-up audit.
rules.nacsaNotificationType: MAP status update - high finding remediation delay

rules.trigger: Medium finding overdue by 1-60 calendar days
rules.action: Internal escalation to MAP owner for revised timeline and prioritization review

rules.trigger: Medium finding overdue by more than 60 calendar days
rules.escalateTo: Chief Executive Officer / Head of Entity
rules.action: Written escalation to entity head. Finding will be carried forward to next audit cycle.

rules.trigger: Any finding deferred without formal approval
rules.escalateTo: Chief Executive Officer / Head of Entity
rules.action: Immediate escalation. Deferral requires formal management approval and risk acceptance statement.
rules.nacsaNotificationType: Unauthorized deferral of audit finding remediation

rules.trigger: Critical or High finding deferred for any reason
rules.action: NACSA notification required. Deferral of critical/high findings must include compensating controls and formal risk acceptance by entity board.
rules.nacsaNotificationType: Deferral of critical/high severity finding - requires NACSA review

reviewCadence.criticalFindings: Weekly review by MAP owner; fortnightly reporting to entity head
reviewCadence.highFindings: Fortnightly review by MAP owner; monthly reporting to entity head
reviewCadence.nacsaReporting: Quarterly MAP status report submitted to NACSA as per Direction No. 8

summaryDashboard.description: Aggregated view of MAP progress for management reporting and NACSA submissions.

remediationProgress.guidance: Track remediation progress over time to identify trends.
remediationProgress.projectedCompletionDate: [YYYY-MM-DD - estimated date when all findings will be remediated based on current trajectory]

upcomingDeadlines.guidance: List MAP items with target completion dates within the next 30 days.

nacsaSubmission.description: Structure for quarterly MAP status submissions to NACSA as required by Direction No. 8.

keyAchievements: [Summary of significant remediation milestones achieved in the reporting period]
supportRequested: [Any support or guidance requested from NACSA]

attachments: Evidence of completion for items marked as Completed in this period
Deferral justification forms (if any deferrals in this period)
